name: Parisian Hearts
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 ounces Blanched whole almonds, finely ground
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine flour, ground almonds, butter or margarine, almond extract, salt, egg, and 1/2 cup confectioners' sugar. Beat ingredients with a mixer on low speed until well blended, scraping the bowl occasionally with a rubber spatula.
2. Shape the dough into a ball and wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for 2 hours or until easy to handle.
3. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Grease two large cookie sheets.
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out half of the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch using a lightly floured rolling pin. Keep the remaining dough refrigerated.
6. Using a 3-1/2-inch heart-shaped cookie cutter, cut the rolled dough into hearts. Alternatively, cut a 3-1/2-inch heart-shaped pattern from cardboard and use it with a knife to cut out cookies.
7. With a floured 1-inch round cookie cutter, cut out the center of half of the hearts.
8. Place the hearts on the prepared cookie sheets, spacing them about 1/2 inch apart using a pancake turner.
9. Bake for 10 minutes or until golden brown. Transfer cookies immediately to wire racks to cool.
10. Repeat with the remaining dough and trimmings.
11. To assemble the cookies, stir lemon juice with 1/2 cup confectioners' sugar in a small bowl until smooth. Spread a thin layer of the mixture on the hearts without cut-out centers. Place each heart with a cut-out center gently over the filled hearts, pressing them together lightly. Fill the cut-out centers with about a quarter teaspoonful of raspberry preserves.
12. Sprinkle the cookies lightly with confectioners' sugar. Store in tightly covered containers and consume within 2 days. Makes 14 cookies.
